statement.title: Statement
statement.content:
Description
An extraterrestrial species has visited the authors of April Fools Day Contest 2025 while they were preparing problems. They call themselves the "Balikons" and expressed interest in chatting with humans. Although they can make sounds that are similar to human speech, they seem to prefer communicating by writing using a variety of media.

![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2095I/8e0ec68863af22664eb62c7b6945ff4217ad9924.png) Figure 1. A Balikon entity communicates with a human linguist (2025, colorized)Fortunately, there is a linguistics expert in the author team. With some effort, they were eventually able to fully understand the Balikon language. The following is a table of some example phrases they translated.

Balikon scriptLatin scriptTranslation![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2095I/449b571d2ba6c01b220b92edb2ceec88282742cd.png)balikonBalikon![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2095I/04ced3f549c4bf027339713cf53213dd0329de59.png)balikong shasthree Balikons![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2095I/3cc61a230bff40b71dd1c4107a5300c10c76bc7f.png)fibucho shezali kin chakog leshasbuy shezali $ ^{\text{∗}} $ for twelve chakos $ ^{\text{†}} $ ![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2095I/b813e66c97acf2e08e060305b87ab8422515098b.png)fibuchog zinfa bashin(I) bought the wrong pen![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2095I/a81fa21800fb10310da9af1a8b60491b7324f880.png)shons ze tes to les konbilo lonshonletashashaleteshatesfive plus seven minus one modulo 998244358 Table 1. A few pieces of text in the Balikon languageAfter some conversations, the Balikons learned about a popular human activity called competitive programming and were excited to try. In fact, they just created their first problem. Please check it out!

$ ^{\text{∗}} $ a dish in traditional Balikon cuisine, similar to meatballs

$ ^{\text{†}} $ currency unit

Input Format
The input contains two integers $ a $ and $ b $ ( $ 1 \le a, b \le 10^9 $ ).

Output Format
Print the sum $ a + b $ .

Examples
Sample 1
Input
shons tes
Output
leshas

Sample 2
Input
leles lonshons
Output
shatas

Sample 3
Input
lonshonletashashaleteshalons shons
Output
lonshonletashashaleteshates

solutions.title: Solution
solutions.content:
The Balikons use base-9 numbers. This can be easily seen from the very important Figure 1 which clearly shows that the species has 9 fingers.
Now we need to know how to read the mysterious script. Except weird diagonal attachments which work as word ending markers, all characters are formed by horizontal and vertical strokes. In fact, each unique set of horizontal or vertical strokes has its own reading, and a character's reading is a defined by its horizontal and vertical components. For more information see Figure 2.
Observe that numbers are denoted by words that end with -s. We can see that characters used as digits all have a specific form: two long horizontal strokes and two long vertical strokes. Also, notice that each Balikon character resembles a 3x3 grid. Combining these and the base-9 fact, we can see that each digit character creates a square on the grid. From all digits in the example (1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7) we can see that the numbers are ordered from top to bottom from left to right.
Figure 2: Balikon script
Now we know all digits in the Balikon script and how to read them.
